stories.title: Judge bars chef Eric Adjepong from being alone with daughter after abuse finding
stories.summary: A Maryland judge has issued a protective order against chef and TV host Eric Adjepong, prohibiting him from contacting his 8-year-old daughter without supervision until he completes parenting classes and reunification therapy. The order was issued after a judge determined there was evidence of physical abuse. Adjepong is barred from contact with his daughter until July 10, 2027. He will have to comply with the order before he can be alone with his child again.

stories.title: Cubs, Tigers face off in rubber match (8:10 p.m. ET on ESPN)
stories.summary: The Chicago Cubs face the Detroit Tigers in a rubber match on ESPN at 8:10 p.m. ET. The Cubs have had a mixed season, with two 10-game winning streaks and a 10-game losing streak. Pete Crow-Armstrong, a 24-year-old outfielder, has been a key contributor with a .905 OPS, 21 home runs, and 25 stolen bases. The winner of this matchup will take the series.

stories.title: Dodgers look to take series from Phillies
stories.summary: The Dodgers aim to take the series from the Phillies in Philadelphia, with Eric Lauer on the mound for the third game. Lauer has allowed three earned runs or fewer in seven consecutive starts. The Phillies' Trea Turner has a six-game hit streak and is 6-for-16 against Lauer, including two home runs. The outcome will be determined by how the Phillies' offense responds to Lauer's pitching style.

stories.title: Lego’s Donkey Kong arcade machine lets Mario jump endless barrels — Miyamoto is reportedly happy
stories.summary: A Lego Donkey Kong arcade machine has been created, priced at $200, featuring a working joystick and button to control Mario's movement and jumping. The game includes an endless series of barrels for Mario to jump, utilizing the same original barrels first released in 1989 for Lego's pirate sets. The machine's design has reportedly won the approval of Mario creator Shigeru Miyamoto. The set is now available for purchase.

stories.title: China's Moonshot AI stole from Anthropic, Trump tech adviser says
stories.summary: China's Moonshot AI has allegedly stolen capabilities from US AI model Fable, developed by Anthropic, according to a White House adviser. Michael Kratsios claims Moonshot used distillation to extract answers from Fable, and gained access to restricted Nvidia servers to train its K3 model. The US government has information on this alleged theft, which occurred before the unveiling of Moonshot's K3 model last week. The US restricted Nvidia's most advanced chip exports in 2022 due to similar concerns.

stories.title: As Sephora Seeks Active Beauty's Next Stars, WNBA Player Lexie Hull's Forta Bounces Into Accelerate
stories.summary: WNBA player Lexie Hull's makeup line Forta Cosmetics has seen significant success, with its initial product, a sweat-resistant setting spray, generating over $10,000 in sales within 24 hours. The brand, co-founded by Hull and her former Stanford roommate Sarah Guller, targets active women who need makeup that can withstand various activities. Forta Cosmetics aims to provide a seamless makeup experience for women on-the-go. The company is now seeking to expand its product line to meet the growing demand.

stories.title: Where to Find a Commonwealth Games 2026 Livestream in the U.S. to Watch the Event for Free
stories.summary: The 2026 Commonwealth Games will take place in Glasgow, Scotland, from July 23 to August 2, featuring over 7,000 athletes from more than 70 nations. The event will air live on CBC in Canada, and viewers in the US can watch for free by using a virtual private network to access the CBC's coverage. This is the 17th edition of the quadrennial event, which dates back to 1930. The Games will be available to stream online from anywhere in the world.

stories.title: Even camels can't cope: Africa's ships of the desert hit by rising temperatures
stories.summary: Rising temperatures in Africa's Afar region are causing severe heat stress in camels, with herder Ali Umer reporting eight camel calves lost in the past month due to blistering feet, watery eyes, and skin burns. The region is one of the hottest and driest places on Earth, where nomads lead camel caravans carrying salt. Temperatures are becoming increasingly intense, with even the wind now carrying heat. Herders are struggling to cope with the extreme conditions.

stories.title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The US and Saudi Arabia have signed a landmark nuclear deal, a "peaceful nuclear co-operation agreement" that could allow Saudi Arabia to enrich uranium in the future. The deal, announced on July 22, includes a bilateral safeguards agreement to prevent nuclear proliferation. The partnership is expected to be a multi-billion-dollar, decades-long agreement. The US and Saudi Arabia will now work together to advance economic and strategic objectives in the energy sector.

stories.title: Canada's 'powerful' dairy sector is in Trump's trade crosshairs
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has targeted Canada's dairy supply management system, imposing a 50% tariff on $20 billion worth of Canadian goods imported to the US, set to take effect in August. The system, which includes production quotas and set pricing, is seen as a major irritant by American farmers. Canadian politicians, including Quebec Premier Christine Fréchette, have indicated that dairy supply management is non-negotiable. The move is likely to escalate tensions between the two countries.

stories.title: Republican Lawmaker Urges Charges Against Jack Smith
stories.summary: The criminal referral could provide a catalyst for a prosecution of Mr. Smith, who as special counsel became a top target of the president’s retribution campaign.

stories.title: Three Airports Plan to Ditch T.S.A. Agents Amid Push for Private Security
stories.summary: The airports have opted in to the agency’s new security model, an agency spokesperson confirmed on Wednesday. A union official said the move could jeopardize public safety.

stories.title: Houthis Claim Strikes on 2 Saudi Oil Tankers in Red Sea
stories.summary: The Saudi authorities had yet to confirm the hits, which would be the first attacks since the Iranian-backed Yemeni group announced a blockade on Saudi Arabia.

stories.title: Most sewage spills in England may be illegal, new research suggests
stories.summary: New research suggests that nearly 9 in 10 hours of raw sewage spills in England may be illegal, with 86% of incidents occurring without heavy rain. The analysis, covering over 350 storm overflow outlets, found that water companies serving more than 30 million people failed to meet regulations. This raises concerns about the legitimacy of sewage spills, with none of the water companies performing well in the study. The findings may lead to increased scrutiny and enforcement of water companies' compliance with environmental regulations.

stories.title: Hope for thousands of MS patients as 'life-changing' drug now on NHS in England
stories.summary: Up to 5,000 people in England with multiple sclerosis will now have access to the life-changing drug fampridine, which boosts nerve signals to help them walk, after it was made available on the NHS. The drug, already available elsewhere in the UK, can improve walking speed and distance for nearly four in 10 users. This decision could significantly impact the lives of those with MS, enabling them to live independently and participate in daily activities. The NHS will continue to provide physiotherapy and walking aids to support patients.

stories.title: Airport drop-off fees up by a third - here are the priciest
stories.summary: Drop-off fees at the UK's major airports have increased by a third on average since last summer, with two London airports, Gatwick and Stansted, charging the highest fees at £10. The fees are part of airports' business models and climate change objectives, with some airports offering free drop-offs. London City Airport now charges £8 for five minutes, making it the most expensive on a per-minute basis. As a result, Gatwick is pushing for more passengers to arrive by trains and buses.

stories.title: House Passes Defense Bill Amid Iran War Divide
stories.summary: Republicans pushed through the $1.15 trillion bill over opposition from Democrats, who said backing it was akin to embracing the war.

stories.title: McInnes glad of Rangers backing in appeal against ban
stories.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has received a four-match touchline ban for criticizing a late penalty awarded to Celtic at Motherwell in May. The comments were made when McInnes was in charge of Hearts, and Rangers are now contesting the ban. The Scottish FA had initially imposed a three-game ban with one suspended until the end of the season. McInnes is glad Rangers are appealing the decision, which he believes was wrong.

stories.title: Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs
stories.summary: Google's parent company Alphabet reported a negative free cash flow of $5.9 billion for the first time in at least a decade, due to soaring spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure. The company's AI costs are expected to reach $205 billion this year, up from $190 billion. This significant increase in AI spending has led to a decline in Alphabet's stock, falling 4% in after-hours trading. The company's quarterly revenue did rise 23% to $119.8 billion.

stories.title: Yeon Sang-ho Returns to ‘Parasyte’ Universe With ‘Tamiya,’ Starring Shioli Kutsuna, at Netflix
stories.summary: South Korean filmmaker Yeon Sang-ho is returning to the "Parasyte" universe with a new live-action series, "Parasyte: Tamiya," starring Shioli Kutsuna, set to stream on Netflix in 2027. This marks the third collaboration between Yeon and Netflix, following "Hellbound" and "Parasyte: The Grey." The series is developed by Wowpoint, a production company behind Yeon's previous works. Production on "Parasyte: Tamiya" is currently underway.

stories.title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
stories.summary: Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.

stories.title: Tears and sleepless nights as Marshall and Adlington get Scotland flag honour
stories.summary: Scottish athletes Marshall and Adlington have been chosen to carry the Scotland flag at the Commonwealth Games. Marshall, a 59-year-old bowler, and Adlington, a 39-year-old judo athlete, will share the honour. The event marks a special moment for Marshall, who will be joined by his niece in the bowls team. The flag will be returned to its owners after the ceremony, but Marshall jokingly claims he'd win a battle with Adlington for first shot at it.

stories.title: Liverpool confident of signing Barcola - Thursday's gossip
stories.summary: Liverpool is confident of signing Paris St-Germain forward Bradley Barcola, a 23-year-old who impressed with four goal contributions at the World Cup with France this summer. The move would come after beating Arsenal and Bayern Munich to the signature. Barcola's World Cup performance has sparked interest from multiple top clubs. Liverpool's bid for Barcola is expected to be a closely watched transfer saga in the coming weeks.

stories.title: Shaken, Soaked and Seasick: What It’s Like to Survive ‘The Odyssey’ in 4DX
stories.summary: A 3-hour screening of Christopher Nolan's "The Odyssey" in 4DX format left some viewers shaken, soaked, and seasick. The immersive experience features seat-rumbling and water-spritzing technology at select theaters. Over 100 people reportedly experienced motion sickness during the film's premiere. Theaters are now warning patrons about the potential for discomfort.

stories.title: Alphabet Quadruples Profit to $112 Billion, Fueled by A.I. Investments
stories.summary: Google’s parent company, which has spent heavily on artificial intelligence, also showed growth across its businesses, particularly in cloud computing.

stories.title: What Were the Lotus-Eaters Eating in Homer’s ‘Odyssey’?
stories.summary: In Homer’s epic, Odysseus’s men eat something so powerful that they forget about home. Which plants could have produced such a soporific, narcotic and amnesiac effect?

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health committee report finds obesity is an engineered outcome, not a failure of willpower, with evidence pointing to sugar and refined carbohydrates as primary drivers. The report criticizes the "high in fat, salt or sugar" framework, which it says is flawed and encourages manufacturers to replace fat with sweet-tasting substitutes. The framework has been used in British food policy for decades. The committee is now calling for supermarkets and manufacturers to be held accountable for reducing sugar in products.

He will have to comply with the order before he can be alone with his child again.
